2604.18187.title: Audio-DeepThinker: Progressive Reasoning-Aware Reinforcement Learning for High-Quality Chain-of-Thought Emergence in Audio Language Models
2604.18187.paper_summary: Large Audio-Language Models (LALMs) have made significant progress in audio understanding, yet they primarily operate as perception-and-answer systems without explicit reasoning processes. Existing methods for enhancing audio reasoning rely either on supervised chain-of-thought (CoT) fine-tuning, which is limited by training data quality, or on reinforcement learning (RL) with coarse rewards that do not directly evaluate reasoning quality. As a result, the generated reasoning chains often appear well-structured yet lack specific acoustic grounding. We propose Audio-DeepThinker, a framework built on two core ideas. First, we introduce a hybrid reasoning similarity reward that directly supervises the quality of generated reasoning chains by combining an LLM evaluator assessing logical path alignment, key step coverage, and analytical depth with an embedding similarity component enforcing semantic alignment with reference reasoning chains. Second, we propose a progressive two-stage curriculum that enables high-quality CoT reasoning to emerge through pure RL exploration, without any supervised reasoning fine-tuning, from an instruction-tuned model that possesses no prior chain-of-thought capability. Stage 1 trains on foundational audio QA with the hybrid reward to foster basic reasoning patterns, while Stage 2 shifts to acoustically challenging boundary cases with an LLM-only reward for greater reasoning diversity. Audio-DeepThinker achieves state-of-the-art results on MMAR (74.0%), MMAU-test-mini (78.5%), and MMSU (77.26%), winning 1st Place in the Interspeech 2026 Audio Reasoning Challenge (Single Model Track). Interpretability analyses further reveal that RL training primarily reshapes upper-layer MoE gating mechanisms and that reasoning tokens crystallize progressively in the upper transformer layers, offering mechanistic insights into how audio reasoning emerges through exploration.
2604.18187.paper_summary_zh: 大型音訊語言模型（LALMs）在音訊理解方面取得了顯著進展，但它們主要作為感知與回答系統運作，並不具備明確的推理過程。現有的增強音訊推理方法要麼依賴於受監督的思維鏈（CoT）微調，這受到訓練數據質量的限制，要麼依賴於強化學習（RL），使用粗略的獎勵來評估推理質量。結果是，生成的推理鏈雖然結構良好，但缺乏具體的音響基礎。我們提出了Audio-DeepThinker，這是一個建立在兩個核心思想上的框架。首先，我們引入了一種混合推理相似性獎勵，通過結合評估邏輯路徑對齊、關鍵步驟覆蓋和分析深度的LLM評估器與強制語義對齊的嵌入相似性組件，直接監督生成推理鏈的質量。其次，我們提出了一個漸進的兩階段課程，通過純粹的RL探索使高質量的CoT推理得以出現，而無需任何受監督的推理微調，從一個沒有先前思維鏈能力的指令調整模型中開始。第一階段在基礎音訊QA上進行訓練，使用混合獎勵以促進基本推理模式，而第二階段則轉向具有音響挑戰性的邊界案例，使用僅限LLM的獎勵以獲得更大的推理多樣性。Audio-DeepThinker在MMAR（74.0%）、MMAU-test-mini（78.5%）和MMSU（77.26%）上達到了最先進的結果，並在2026年Interspeech音訊推理挑戰賽（單模型賽道）中獲得第一名。可解釋性分析進一步揭示，RL訓練主要重塑了上層MoE閘控機制，推理標記在上層Transformer層中逐漸結晶，提供了音訊推理如何通過探索出現的機械性見解。
2604.18187.author: Xiang He et.al.
2604.18187.authors: Xiang He, Chenxing Li, Jinting Wang, Yan Rong, Tianxin Xie, Wenfu Wang, Li Liu, Dong Yu
